name: OBD-II Overview
title: On-Board Diagnostics II (OBD-II / EOBD) Overview
intro: OBD-II (US, 1996+) / EOBD (EU, 2001+) is a regulatory diagnostic interface for emission-related components.
OBD-II defines PIDs (Parameter IDs) for live data, Mode 03 for stored DTCs, Mode 04 for clearing, Mode 06 for test results, Mode 07 for pending DTCs, Mode 09 for vehicle info.

paragraphs: All OBD-II compliant ECUs must support the standard PIDs in each mode.
tables: 0x0C = Engine RPM, 0x0D = Vehicle Speed, 0x0F = Intake Air Temp
Returns stored DTCs (e.g. P0301 = Cyl 1 Misfire)
Clears DTCs and freeze frames; also resets readiness monitors
